args.code:
import json
import re

# Access the articles data from previous query
articles_data = var_functions.query_db:2

# Define sports-related keywords to identify sports articles
sports_keywords = [
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis',
    'golf', 'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling',
    'olympic', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'league',
    'game', 'match', 'player', 'team', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat',
    'coach', 'season', 'sport', 'athlete', 'medal', 'title', 'cup',
    'final', 'quarter', 'semifinal', 'quarterfinal', 'racing', 'marathon'
]

# Function to check if an article is about sports
def is_sports_article(title, description):
    title_lower = title.lower()
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    # Check if any sports keyword appears in title or description
    for keyword in sports_keywords:
        if keyword in title_lower or keyword in desc_lower:
            return True
    return False

# Process articles to find sports articles and their description lengths
sports_articles = []

for article in articles_data:
    title = article.get('title', '')
    description = article.get('description', '')
    
    if is_sports_article(title, description):
        desc_length = len(description)
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': title,
            'description': description,
            'desc_length': desc_length,
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort sports articles by description length (descending)
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Get the sports article with the longest description
if sports_articles_sorted:
    longest_article = sports_articles_sorted[0]
    result = {
        'title': longest_article['title'],
        'description_length': longest_article['desc_length'],
        'total_sports_articles_found': len(sports_articles)
    }
else:
    result = {
        'message': 'No sports articles found',
        'total_sports_articles_found': 0
    }

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

args.code:
import json

# The var_functions.query_db:10 contains the actual articles data, not a file path
# Let's access it directly
all_articles = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']

# Process articles to find sports articles
sports_keywords = [
    'game', 'games', 'season', 'player', 'players', 'team', 'teams', 'coach', 
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'athlete', 'athletes',
    'championship', 'tournament', 'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat',
    'medal', 'olympic', 'olympics', 'world cup', 'league', 'cup', 'final', 'semifinal',
    'quarterfinal', 'racing', 'marathon', 'sports', 'sport'
]

# Function to check if article is about sports
def is_sports_article(title, description):
    text = f"{title} {description}".lower()
    return any(keyword in text for keyword in sports_keywords)

# Find sports articles and their description lengths
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    title = article.get('title', '')
    description = article.get('description', '')
    
    if is_sports_article(title, description):
        desc_length = len(description)
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': title,
            'description': description,
            'desc_length': desc_length,
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length (descending)
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Create result
result = {
    'total_articles_processed': len(all_articles),
    'sports_articles_found': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'all_sports_articles': [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in sports_articles_sorted[:10]]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json

# The var_functions.query_db:10 contains the actual articles data, not a file path
# Let's access it directly
all_articles = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']

# Process articles to find sports articles
sports_keywords = [
    'game', 'games', 'season', 'player', 'players', 'team', 'teams', 'coach', 
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'athlete', 'athletes',
    'championship', 'tournament', 'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat',
    'medal', 'olympic', 'olympics', 'world cup', 'league', 'cup', 'final', 'semifinal',
    'quarterfinal', 'racing', 'marathon', 'sports', 'sport'
]

# Function to check if article is about sports
def is_sports_article(title, description):
    text = f"{title} {description}".lower()
    return any(keyword in text for keyword in sports_keywords)

# Find sports articles and their description lengths
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    title = article.get('title', '')
    description = article.get('description', '')
    
    if is_sports_article(title, description):
        desc_length = len(description)
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': title,
            'description': description,
            'desc_length': desc_length,
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length (descending)
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Create result
result = {
    'total_articles_processed': len(all_articles),
    'sports_articles_found': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'all_sports_articles': [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in sports_articles_sorted[:10]]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

A lot of developers shorten this to:\\\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( FeedLocator.class );\\\\But this still leaves us with cut and paste errors.\\\\What if we could just reduce it to:\\\\    static Logger logger = Logger.g ...\\\\", "desc_length": 708, "article_id": "172"}}

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Comprehensive sports keywords
sports_keywords = {
    'game', 'games', 'season', 'player', 'players', 'team', 'teams', 'coach', 'coaches',
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf', 'cricket',
    'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'athlete', 'athletes', 'championship',
    'tournament', 'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'medal', 'medals',
    'olympic', 'olympics', 'world cup', 'league', 'cup', 'final', 'semifinal',
    'quarterfinal', 'quarter', 'racing', 'race', 'marathon', 'sports', 'sport',
    'ball', 'win', 'wins', 'won', 'loss', 'record', 'coach', 'season', 'play',
    'playing', 'played', 'competition', 'compete', 'champion', 'title'
}

# Function to identify sports articles
def is_sports_article(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    description = article.get('description', '').lower()
    
    # Check for direct sports indicators
    text = f"{title} {description}"
    
    # Count sports keyword matches (need at least 2 for confidence, or specific sport names)
    matches = sum(1 for keyword in sports_keywords if keyword in text)
    
    # If we find specific sport names or multiple sports terms, it's likely sports
    specific_sports = {'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 
                       'golf', 'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling'}
    
    has_specific_sport = any(sport in text for sport in specific_sports)
    
    return has_specific_sport or matches >= 2

# Find all sports articles with their description lengths
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length (descending)
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Prepare results
result = {
    'total_articles': len(all_articles),
    'sports_articles_found': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

The testers on the Windows team were\\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:

# More strict sports keywords - focus on specific sports and context

    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',

    'olympics', 'olympic', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon',
    'formula 1', 'f1', 'nascar', 'racing'
}

sports_context_words = {
    'season', 'player', 'players', 'team', 'teams', 'coach', 'coaches', 'game',
    'games', 'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'medal', 'medals',
    'champion', 'title', 'league', 'cup', 'final', 'semifinal', 'quarterfinal',
    'quarter', 'half', 'inning', 'innings', 'period', 'round', 'bout',
    'record', 'records', 'win', 'wins', 'won', 'loss', 'beat', 'beats',

}

# Function to identify sports articles more accurately
def is_sports_article_strict(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    description = article.get('description', '').lower()
    
    text = f"{title} {description}"
    
    # Check for specific sport names first (strong indicator)
    has_specific_sport = any(sport in text for sport in specific_sports)
    
    # Count sports context words
    context_matches = sum(1 for word in sports_context_words if word in text)
    
    # Must have either a specific sport name or multiple context words
    return has_specific_sport or context_matches >= 3

# Find sports articles with strict filtering
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article_strict(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length (descending)
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Get top 10 longest sports articles for verification
top_10_longest = sports_articles_sorted[:10]

result = {
    'total_articles': len(all_articles),
    'sports_articles_found': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'top_10_longest': [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in top_10_longest]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

longest_sports_article.title: Pretty Log4J
longest_sports_article.description: \\I've been a big fan of Log4J  for a while now but haven't migrated any code\over for one central reason.  The following line of code:\\    final static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "some.name" );\\... is amazingly ugly and difficult to work with.\\Most people use Log4J with a logger based on the classname:\\So we would probably see:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "org.apache.commons.feedparser.locate.FeedLocator" );\\Which is amazingly verbose.  A lot of developers shorten this to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( FeedLocator.class );\\But this still leaves us with cut and paste errors.\\What if we could just reduce it to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.g ...\\

    'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding', 'skating', 'fencing',
    'olympics', 'olympic', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon',
    'formula 1', 'f1', 'nascar', 'racing'
}

sports_context_words = {
    'season', 'player', 'players', 'team', 'teams', 'coach', 'coaches', 'game',
    'games', 'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'medal', 'medals',
    'champion', 'title', 'league', 'cup', 'final', 'semifinal', 'quarterfinal',
    'quarter', 'half', 'inning', 'innings', 'period', 'round', 'bout',
    'record', 'records', 'win', 'wins', 'won', 'loss', 'beat', 'beats',
    'athlete', 'athletes', 'competition', 'compete', 'competing'
}

# Function to identify sports articles more accurately
def is_sports_article_strict(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    description = article.get('description', '').lower()
    
    text = f"{title} {description}"

    has_specific_sport = any(sport in text for sport in specific_sports)
    
    # Count sports context words

    # Must have either a specific sport name or multiple context words
    return has_specific_sport or context_matches >= 3

# Find sports articles with strict filtering
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article_strict(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length (descending)
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Get top 10 longest sports articles for verification
top_10_longest = sports_articles_sorted[:10]

result = {
    'total_articles': len(all_articles),

    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'top_10_longest': [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in top_10_longest]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

longest_sports_article.title: Why Windows isn't Unix
longest_sports_article.description: \\"I first heard about this from one of the developers of the hit game SimCity, who\told me that there was a critical bug in his application: it used memory right\after freeing it, a major no-no that happened to work OK on DOS but would not\work under Windows where memory that is freed is likely to be snatched up by\another running application right away. The testers on the Windows team were\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. They reported this to the Windows\developers, who disassembled SimCity, stepped through it in a debugger, found\the bug, and added special code that checked if SimCity was running, and if it\did ...\\

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Sports-specific keywords for accurate classification
sports_terms = {
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'running', 'track', 'field', 'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing',
    'snowboarding', 'skating', 'fencing', 'olympics', 'olympic', 'nba', 'nfl',
    'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon',
    'formula 1', 'f1', 'nascar', 'racing', 'race', 'relay', 'medal', 'gold',
    'silver', 'bronze', 'quarterfinal', 'semifinal', 'final', 'coach', 'season'
}

sports_context = {
    'team', 'teams', 'player', 'players', 'game', 'games', 'match', 'matches',
    'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'win', 'wins', 'won', 'loss', 'beat',
    'athlete', 'athletes', 'competition'
}

def is_sports_article(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    text = f"{title} {desc}"
    
    # Check for direct sports terms
    has_sport_term = any(term in text for term in sports_terms)
    
    # Count context words
    context_count = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in text)
    
    # Must have either a sport term or multiple context words
    return has_sport_term or context_count >= 2

# Find all sports articles
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
def sort_by_length(articles):
    return sorted(articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

sports_sorted = sort_by_length(sports_articles)

# Get the articles with longest descriptions
top_articles = sports_sorted[:5]
all_titles_with_lengths = [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in sports_sorted[:20]]

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_article': sports_sorted[0] if sports_sorted else None,
    'top_5_longest': top_articles,
    'sample_titles_with_lengths': all_titles_with_lengths
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

longest_sports_article.title: Pretty Log4J
longest_sports_article.description: \\I've been a big fan of Log4J  for a while now but haven't migrated any code\over for one central reason.  The following line of code:\\    final static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "some.name" );\\... is amazingly ugly and difficult to work with.\\Most people use Log4J with a logger based on the classname:\\So we would probably see:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "org.apache.commons.feedparser.locate.FeedLocator" );\\Which is amazingly verbose.  A lot of developers shorten this to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( FeedLocator.class );\\But this still leaves us with cut and paste errors.\\What if we could just reduce it to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.g ...\\

longest_sports_article.title: Why Windows isn't Unix
longest_sports_article.description: \\"I first heard about this from one of the developers of the hit game SimCity, who\told me that there was a critical bug in his application: it used memory right\after freeing it, a major no-no that happened to work OK on DOS but would not\work under Windows where memory that is freed is likely to be snatched up by\another running application right away. The testers on the Windows team were\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. They reported this to the Windows\developers, who disassembled SimCity, stepped through it in a debugger, found\the bug, and added special code that checked if SimCity was running, and if it\did ...\\

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Define precise sports indicators
sports_indicators = {
    # Specific sports (strongest indicator)
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'track', 'field', 'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding',
    'skating', 'fencing', 'rowing', 'sailing', 'shooting', 'archery', 'badminton',
    'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'taekwondo', 'triathlon', 'weightlifting',
    
    # Sports organizations and events
    'olympics', 'olympic', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon',
    'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'formula 1', 'f1', 'ufc', 'ncaa',
    'ryder cup', 'super bowl', 'playoff', 'playoffs', 'finals', 'world series',
    'wimbledon', 'us open', 'masters', 'pga tour', 'world championship',
    
    # Sports roles in specific contexts
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'goalie', 'defender',
    'forward', 'center', 'guard', 'shooting guard', 'point guard', 'running back',
    'wide receiver', 'linebacker', 'shortstop', 'outfielder', 'first baseman'
}

# Additional context words that only count if we have sports context
sports_context = {
    'team', 'teams', 'player', 'players', 'coach', 'season', 'game', 'games',
    'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'win', 'wins', 'won',
    'loss', 'beat', 'medal', 'medals', 'champion', 'title', 'league', 'cup',
    'final', 'semifinal', 'quarterfinal', 'record', 'records', 'athlete'
}

def is_sports_article_strict(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    text = f"{title} {desc}"
    
    # Check for direct sports indicators
    has_sport_indicator = any(indicator in text for indicator in sports_indicators)
    
    # Count context words (need at least 2 to support)
    context_count = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in text)
    
    # Must have a specific sport indicator OR (context words >= 3)
    return has_sport_indicator or context_count >= 3

# Find sports articles
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article_strict(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Get the article with longest description
longest_article = sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': longest_article,
    'top_10_by_length': [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in sports_articles_sorted[:10]]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

val_args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Define precise sports indicators
sports_indicators = {
    # Specific sports (strongest indicator)
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'track', 'field', 'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding',
    'skating', 'fencing', 'rowing', 'sailing', 'shooting', 'archery', 'badminton',
    'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'taekwondo', 'triathlon', 'weightlifting',
    
    # Sports organizations and events
    'olympics', 'olympic', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon',
    'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'formula 1', 'f1', 'ufc', 'ncaa',
    'ryder cup', 'super bowl', 'playoff', 'playoffs', 'finals', 'world series',
    'wimbledon', 'us open', 'masters', 'pga tour', 'world championship',
    
    # Sports roles in specific contexts
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'goalie', 'defender',
    'forward', 'center', 'guard', 'shooting guard', 'point guard', 'running back',
    'wide receiver', 'linebacker', 'shortstop', 'outfielder', 'first baseman'
}

# Additional context words that only count if we have sports context
sports_context = {
    'team', 'teams', 'player', 'players', 'coach', 'season', 'game', 'games',
    'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'win', 'wins', 'won',
    'loss', 'beat', 'medal', 'medals', 'champion', 'title', 'league', 'cup',
    'final', 'semifinal', 'quarterfinal', 'record', 'records', 'athlete'
}

def is_sports_article_strict(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    text = f"{title} {desc}"
    
    # Check for direct sports indicators
    has_sport_indicator = any(indicator in text for indicator in sports_indicators)
    
    # Count context words (need at least 2 to support)
    context_count = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in text)
    
    # Must have a specific sport indicator OR (context words >= 3)
    return has_sport_indicator or context_count >= 3

# Find sports articles
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article_strict(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Get the article with longest description
longest_article = sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': longest_article,
    'top_10_by_length': [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in sports_articles_sorted[:10]]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Sports keywords that actually indicate sports content (not gaming/figurative)
sports_keywords = {
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'track and field', 'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding',
    'skating', 'fencing', 'rowing', 'sailing', 'shooting', 'archery', 'badminton',
    'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'taekwondo', 'triathlon', 'weightlifting',
    'olympics', 'olympic', 'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'ufc',
    'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon', 'ryder cup', 'super bowl',
    'playoff', 'playoffs', 'finals', 'world series', 'wimbledon', 'u.s. open',
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'goalie', 'defender',
    'forward', 'center', 'guard', 'running back', 'wide receiver', 'shortstop',
    'outfielder', 'first baseman', 'coach', 'coaches', 'quarterfinal', 'semifinal'
}

# Context words that suggest actual sports (need multiple or strong context)
sports_context = {
    'season', 'team', 'teams', 'player', 'players', 'game', 'games', 'match',
    'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'win', 'wins', 'won', 'loss',
    'beat', 'medal', 'medals', 'champion', 'title', 'league', 'cup', 'final',
    'record', 'records', 'athlete', 'athletes', 'competition', 'arena', 'stadium'
}

def get_sports_articles():
    sports_articles = []
    
    for article in all_articles:
        title = article.get('title', '').lower()
        desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
        text = f"{title} {desc}"
        
        # Check for direct sport mentions
        direct_sport = any(kw in text for kw in sports_keywords)
        
        # Count context words
        context_matches = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in text)
        
        # Filter: must have direct sport OR (strong context + at least 1 sport keyword)
        # Also exclude articles about video games, business teams, etc.
        
        # Exclude video game articles
        video_game_terms = ['video game', 'computer game', 'gaming', 'playstation', 
                           'xbox', 'nintendo', 'simcity', 'madden']
        if any(vg in text for vg in video_game_terms):
            continue
            
        # Exclude business/sales team articles
        business_terms = ['sales team', 'business team', 'project team', 'development team',
                         'software', 'programming', 'chips', 'processor', 'computer']
        if any(biz in text for biz in business_terms):
            # But allow if it also has strong sports indicators
            if not direct_sport:
                continue
        
        if direct_sport or (context_matches >= 2 and any(word in text for word in ['season', 'team', 'player', 'game', 'match', 'championship', 'tournament'])):
            sports_articles.append({
                'title': article.get('title'),
                'description': article.get('description'),
                'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
                'article_id': article.get('article_id')
            })
    
    return sports_articles

# Get sports articles
sports_articles = get_sports_articles()

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Sample some articles to verify they're actually sports

The following line of code:\\    final static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "some.name" );\\... is amazingly ugly and difficult to work with.\\Most people use Log4J with a logger based on the classname:\\So we would probably see:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "org.apache.commons.feedparser.locate.FeedLocator" );\\Which is amazingly verbose.  A lot of developers shorten this to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( FeedLocator.class );\\But this still leaves us with cut and paste errors.\\What if we could just reduce it to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.g ...\\

longest_sports_article.title: Why Windows isn't Unix
longest_sports_article.description: \\"I first heard about this from one of the developers of the hit game SimCity, who\told me that there was a critical bug in his application: it used memory right\after freeing it, a major no-no that happened to work OK on DOS but would not\work under Windows where memory that is freed is likely to be snatched up by\another running application right away. The testers on the Windows team were\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. They reported this to the Windows\developers, who disassembled SimCity, stepped through it in a debugger, found\the bug, and added special code that checked if SimCity was running, and if it\did ...\\

val_args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Sports keywords that actually indicate sports content (not gaming/figurative)
sports_keywords = {
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'track and field', 'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding',
    'skating', 'fencing', 'rowing', 'sailing', 'shooting', 'archery', 'badminton',
    'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'taekwondo', 'triathlon', 'weightlifting',
    'olympics', 'olympic', 'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'ufc',
    'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon', 'ryder cup', 'super bowl',
    'playoff', 'playoffs', 'finals', 'world series', 'wimbledon', 'u.s. open',
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'goalie', 'defender',
    'forward', 'center', 'guard', 'running back', 'wide receiver', 'shortstop',
    'outfielder', 'first baseman', 'coach', 'coaches', 'quarterfinal', 'semifinal'
}

# Context words that suggest actual sports (need multiple or strong context)
sports_context = {
    'season', 'team', 'teams', 'player', 'players', 'game', 'games', 'match',
    'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'win', 'wins', 'won', 'loss',
    'beat', 'medal', 'medals', 'champion', 'title', 'league', 'cup', 'final',
    'record', 'records', 'athlete', 'athletes', 'competition', 'arena', 'stadium'
}

def get_sports_articles():
    sports_articles = []
    
    for article in all_articles:
        title = article.get('title', '').lower()
        desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
        text = f"{title} {desc}"
        
        # Check for direct sport mentions
        direct_sport = any(kw in text for kw in sports_keywords)
        
        # Count context words
        context_matches = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in text)
        
        # Filter: must have direct sport OR (strong context + at least 1 sport keyword)
        # Also exclude articles about video games, business teams, etc.
        
        # Exclude video game articles
        video_game_terms = ['video game', 'computer game', 'gaming', 'playstation', 
                           'xbox', 'nintendo', 'simcity', 'madden']
        if any(vg in text for vg in video_game_terms):
            continue
            
        # Exclude business/sales team articles
        business_terms = ['sales team', 'business team', 'project team', 'development team',
                         'software', 'programming', 'chips', 'processor', 'computer']
        if any(biz in text for biz in business_terms):
            # But allow if it also has strong sports indicators
            if not direct_sport:
                continue
        
        if direct_sport or (context_matches >= 2 and any(word in text for word in ['season', 'team', 'player', 'game', 'match', 'championship', 'tournament'])):
            sports_articles.append({
                'title': article.get('title'),
                'description': article.get('description'),
                'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
                'article_id': article.get('article_id')
            })
    
    return sports_articles

# Get sports articles
sports_articles = get_sports_articles()

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Sample some articles to verify they're actually sports
sample_articles = [(a['title'], a['desc_length']) for a in sports_articles_sorted[:15]]

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'sample_sports_articles': sample_articles
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

top_5_longest.title: Why Windows isn't Unix
top_5_longest.description: \\"I first heard about this from one of the developers of the hit game SimCity, who\told me that there was a critical bug in his application: it used memory right\after freeing it, a major no-no that happened to work OK on DOS but would not\work under Windows where memory that is freed is likely to be snatched up by\another running application right away. The testers on the Windows team were\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. They reported this to the Windows\developers, who disassembled SimCity, stepped through it in a debugger, found\the bug, and added special code that checked if SimCity was running, and if it\did ...\\

top_5_longest.title: I Confess.  I'm a Software Pirate.
top_5_longest.description: \\I'm guilty.  I'm a Software Pirate!  Not just one or two copies of DOS but\probably hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of software.\\Growing up my parents didn't have much money.  Certainly not hundred of\thousands of dollars for me to blow on software.  I was curious and had a\passion for computers that I couldn't control.  I simply wanted to learn and\couldn't afford to pay for software.\\Luckily I did this when I was a kid so hopefully I won't be prosecuted.  I also\believe that everything I did was ethical.  I didn't take any money out of the\hands of the software industry and I've already contributed WAY more to the\industry than a few hundred thousand in software sales.\\This is th ...\\

top_5_longest.title: What would Baby Jesus Think?
top_5_longest.description: \\"On Tuesday, Cheney, serving in his role as president of the Senate, appeared in\the chamber for a photo session. A chance meeting with Sen. Patrick J. Leahy\(Vt.), the ranking Democrat on the Judiciary Committee, became an argument about\Cheney's ties to Halliburton Co., an international energy services corporation,\and President Bush's judicial nominees. The exchange ended when Cheney offered\some crass advice.\\'Fuck yourself,' said the man who is a heartbeat from the presidency."\\Wonder what the moral majority has to say about this?\\It isn't profanity!  Its a freedom praise!\\

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Very specific sports indicators only
sports_indicators = {
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'track', 'field', 'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding',
    'skating', 'fencing', 'rowing', 'sailing', 'shooting', 'archery', 'badminton',
    'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'taekwondo', 'triathlon', 'weightlifting',
    'olympics', 'olympic', 'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'ufc',
    'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon', 'ryder cup', 'super bowl',
    'playoff', 'playoffs', 'finals', 'world series', 'wimbledon', 'u.s. open',
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'goalie', 'defender',
    'forward', 'center', 'guard', 'running back', 'wide receiver', 'linebacker',
    'shortstop', 'outfielder', 'first baseman', 'coach', 'quarterfinal', 'semifinal'
}

# Must have actual sports content, not just figurative language
def is_sports_article_precise(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    text = f"{title} {desc}"
    
    # Check for specific sports indicators
    has_sport_indicator = any(indicator in text for indicator in sports_indicators)
    
    # Must have sports context words along with indicators
    sports_context = {'season', 'team', 'teams', 'player', 'players', 'game', 'games',
                     'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'win', 'wins',
                     'won', 'loss', 'beat', 'medal', 'medals', 'champion', 'title',
                     'league', 'cup', 'final', 'record', 'records', 'athlete', 'athletes'}
    
    context_count = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in text)
    
    # Exclude video games and business context
    exclusions = {'video game', 'computer game', 'gaming', 'simcity', 'madden',
                  'software', 'chips', 'processor', 'computer', 'programming'}
    
    if any(excl in text for excl in exclusions):
        return False
    
    # Must have sport indicator AND at least one context word
    return has_sport_indicator and context_count >= 1

# Find actual sports articles
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article_precise(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Sample to verify these are actual sports articles
sample_articles = []
for i, article in enumerate(sports_articles_sorted[:20]):
    sample_articles.append({
        'rank': i+1,
        'title': article['title'],
        'length': article['desc_length']
    })

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'sample_sports_articles': sample_articles
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

top_5_longest.title: Why Windows isn't Unix
top_5_longest.description: \\"I first heard about this from one of the developers of the hit game SimCity, who\told me that there was a critical bug in his application: it used memory right\after freeing it, a major no-no that happened to work OK on DOS but would not\work under Windows where memory that is freed is likely to be snatched up by\another running application right away. The testers on the Windows team were\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. They reported this to the Windows\developers, who disassembled SimCity, stepped through it in a debugger, found\the bug, and added special code that checked if SimCity was running, and if it\did ...\\

I simply wanted to learn and\couldn't afford to pay for software.\\Luckily I did this when I was a kid so hopefully I won't be prosecuted.  I also\believe that everything I did was ethical. val_args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Very specific sports indicators only
sports_indicators = {
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'track', 'field', 'athletics', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding',
    'skating', 'fencing', 'rowing', 'sailing', 'shooting', 'archery', 'badminton',
    'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'taekwondo', 'triathlon', 'weightlifting',
    'olympics', 'olympic', 'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'ufc',
    'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon', 'ryder cup', 'super bowl',
    'playoff', 'playoffs', 'finals', 'world series', 'wimbledon', 'u.s. open',
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'goalie', 'defender',
    'forward', 'center', 'guard', 'running back', 'wide receiver', 'linebacker',
    'shortstop', 'outfielder', 'first baseman', 'coach', 'quarterfinal', 'semifinal'
}

# Must have actual sports content, not just figurative language
def is_sports_article_precise(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    text = f"{title} {desc}"
    
    # Check for specific sports indicators
    has_sport_indicator = any(indicator in text for indicator in sports_indicators)
    
    # Must have sports context words along with indicators
    sports_context = {'season', 'team', 'teams', 'player', 'players', 'game', 'games',
                     'match', 'matches', 'score', 'victory', 'defeat', 'win', 'wins',
                     'won', 'loss', 'beat', 'medal', 'medals', 'champion', 'title',
                     'league', 'cup', 'final', 'record', 'records', 'athlete', 'athletes'}
    
    context_count = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in text)
    
    # Exclude video games and business context
    exclusions = {'video game', 'computer game', 'gaming', 'simcity', 'madden',
                  'software', 'chips', 'processor', 'computer', 'programming'}
    
    if any(excl in text for excl in exclusions):
        return False
    
    # Must have sport indicator AND at least one context word
    return has_sport_indicator and context_count >= 1

# Find actual sports articles
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_sports_article_precise(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Sample to verify these are actual sports articles
sample_articles = []
for i, article in enumerate(sports_articles_sorted[:20]):
    sample_articles.append({
        'rank': i+1,
        'title': article['title'],
        'length': article['desc_length']
    })

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'sample_sports_articles': sample_articles
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Ultra-specific sports identification
sports_keywords = {
    # Sport names (exact matches for actual sports)
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding', 'fencing', 'rowing', 
    'sailing', 'archery', 'badminton', 'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'triathlon',
    'weightlifting', 'athletics', 'olympics', 'ryder cup', 'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb',
    'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'ufc', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament',
    'marathon', 'super bowl', 'playoff', 'finals', 'wimbledon'
}

# Sports roles/positions (very specific)
sports_roles = {
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'defender', 'forward',
    'center', 'guard', 'running back', 'wide receiver', 'shortstop', 'outfielder'
}

def is_actual_sports_article(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    full_text = f"{title} {desc}"
    
    # Check for sports keywords (prioritize actual sports)
    has_sport = any(sport in full_text for sport in sports_keywords)
    has_role = any(role in full_text for role in sports_roles)
    
    # Sports context indicators
    sports_context = {'team', 'season', 'player', 'game', 'match', 'score', 'victory',
                     'defeat', 'win', 'champion', 'league', 'medal', 'athlete'}
    context_matches = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in full_text)
    
    # EXCLUSION LIST - very strict
    exclusions = {
        'software', 'computer', 'programming', 'chips', 'processor', 'space',
        'astronaut', 'orbit', 'flight', 'virus', 'flu', 'economy', 'recovery',
        'investment', 'trade', 'market', 'stock', 'stocks', 'business', 'financial'
    }
    
    # If any exclusion found, it's not sports (unless very strong sports indicators)
    if any(excl in full_text for excl in exclusions):
        # Require very strong sports evidence to override exclusion
        strong_sports = {'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'football', 'basketball', 
                        'baseball', 'soccer'}
        if not any(strong in full_text for strong in strong_sports):
            return False
    
    # Must have: (sport keyword + at least 1 context) OR (sports role + context) OR (multiple context)
    return (has_sport and context_matches >= 1) or (has_role and context_matches >= 1) or context_matches >= 3

# Find actual sports articles
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_actual_sports_article(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Verify the top results are actually sports
top_verified = []
for i, article in enumerate(sports_articles_sorted[:10]):
    top_verified.append({
        'rank': i+1,
        'title': article['title'],
        'length': article['desc_length']
    })

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'verified_top_10': top_verified
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

longest_sports_article.title: China Begins Countdown for Next Manned Space Flight
longest_sports_article.description: By ELAINE KURTENBACH    SHANGHAI, China (AP) -- Chinese astronauts are in the final stages of preparing for a manned space mission that will orbit the globe 14 times before returning to Earth, a state-run newspaper reported Thursday.    The launch, expected sometime this month, will initially send a manned craft, the Shenzhou 5, into an oval orbit that at its closest will be 125 miles from Earth, the Liberation Daily reported, citing "relevant channels."    After circling the earth several times, the ship will enter an orbit at about 220 miles from earth, the report said...

val_args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Ultra-specific sports identification
sports_keywords = {
    # Sport names (exact matches for actual sports)
    'football', 'soccer', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'cycling', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'snowboarding', 'fencing', 'rowing', 
    'sailing', 'archery', 'badminton', 'handball', 'judo', 'karate', 'triathlon',
    'weightlifting', 'athletics', 'olympics', 'ryder cup', 'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb',
    'nhl', 'pga', 'nascar', 'ufc', 'world cup', 'championship', 'tournament',
    'marathon', 'super bowl', 'playoff', 'finals', 'wimbledon'
}

# Sports roles/positions (very specific)
sports_roles = {
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'defender', 'forward',
    'center', 'guard', 'running back', 'wide receiver', 'shortstop', 'outfielder'
}

def is_actual_sports_article(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    full_text = f"{title} {desc}"
    
    # Check for sports keywords (prioritize actual sports)
    has_sport = any(sport in full_text for sport in sports_keywords)
    has_role = any(role in full_text for role in sports_roles)
    
    # Sports context indicators
    sports_context = {'team', 'season', 'player', 'game', 'match', 'score', 'victory',
                     'defeat', 'win', 'champion', 'league', 'medal', 'athlete'}
    context_matches = sum(1 for word in sports_context if word in full_text)
    
    # EXCLUSION LIST - very strict
    exclusions = {
        'software', 'computer', 'programming', 'chips', 'processor', 'space',
        'astronaut', 'orbit', 'flight', 'virus', 'flu', 'economy', 'recovery',
        'investment', 'trade', 'market', 'stock', 'stocks', 'business', 'financial'
    }
    
    # If any exclusion found, it's not sports (unless very strong sports indicators)
    if any(excl in full_text for excl in exclusions):
        # Require very strong sports evidence to override exclusion
        strong_sports = {'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'football', 'basketball', 
                        'baseball', 'soccer'}
        if not any(strong in full_text for strong in strong_sports):
            return False
    
    # Must have: (sport keyword + at least 1 context) OR (sports role + context) OR (multiple context)
    return (has_sport and context_matches >= 1) or (has_role and context_matches >= 1) or context_matches >= 3

# Find actual sports articles
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_actual_sports_article(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Verify the top results are actually sports
top_verified = []
for i, article in enumerate(sports_articles_sorted[:10]):
    top_verified.append({
        'rank': i+1,
        'title': article['title'],
        'length': article['desc_length']
    })

result = {
    'total_sports_articles': len(sports_articles),
    'longest_sports_article': sports_articles_sorted[0] if sports_articles_sorted else None,
    'verified_top_10': top_verified
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Ultra-strict sports filter
sports_indicators = {
    # Exact sport names
    'football', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf', 'soccer',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'fencing', 'olympics', 'olympic',
    # Sports organizations
    'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'ufc', 'ryder cup', 'world cup',
    'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon'
}

# Sports positions (very specific)
sports_positions = {
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'defender', 'forward',
    'running back', 'wide receiver', 'shortstop'
}

def is_genuine_sports_article(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    full_text = title + " " + desc
    
    # Check for software/computer context to exclude
    tech_context = {'windows', 'software', 'computer', 'programming', 'chips', 
                   'processor', 'unix', 'log4j', 'developer', 'debug'}
    if any(tech in full_text for tech in tech_context):
        return False
    
    # Check for actual sports indicators
    has_sport = any(sport in full_text for sport in sports_indicators)
    has_position = any(pos in full_text for pos in sports_positions)
    
    # Sports context words
    context_words = {'team', 'season', 'player', 'game', 'match', 'coach', 
                    'victory', 'defeat', 'score', 'medal', 'champion', 'title'}
    context_count = sum(1 for word in context_words if word in full_text)
    
    # Must have a sport indicator AND context
    return (has_sport or has_position) and context_count >= 1

# Find genuine sports articles only
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_genuine_sports_article(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Get the genuine sports article with longest description
if sports_articles_sorted:
    longest_sports = sports_articles_sorted[0]
    result = {
        'title': longest_sports['title'],
        'description_length': longest_sports['desc_length']
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No sports articles found'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json

# Load all articles from file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

# Ultra-strict sports filter
sports_indicators = {
    # Exact sport names
    'football', 'basketball', 'baseball', 'hockey', 'tennis', 'golf', 'soccer',
    'cricket', 'rugby', 'volleyball', 'swimming', 'boxing', 'wrestling',
    'softball', 'gymnastics', 'skiing', 'fencing', 'olympics', 'olympic',
    # Sports organizations
    'nba', 'nfl', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'pga', 'ufc', 'ryder cup', 'world cup',
    'championship', 'tournament', 'marathon'
}

# Sports positions (very specific)
sports_positions = {
    'quarterback', 'pitcher', 'catcher', 'goalkeeper', 'defender', 'forward',
    'running back', 'wide receiver', 'shortstop'
}

def is_genuine_sports_article(article):
    title = article.get('title', '').lower()
    desc = article.get('description', '').lower()
    full_text = title + " " + desc
    
    # Check for software/computer context to exclude
    tech_context = {'windows', 'software', 'computer', 'programming', 'chips', 
                   'processor', 'unix', 'log4j', 'developer', 'debug'}
    if any(tech in full_text for tech in tech_context):
        return False
    
    # Check for actual sports indicators
    has_sport = any(sport in full_text for sport in sports_indicators)
    has_position = any(pos in full_text for pos in sports_positions)
    
    # Sports context words
    context_words = {'team', 'season', 'player', 'game', 'match', 'coach', 
                    'victory', 'defeat', 'score', 'medal', 'champion', 'title'}
    context_count = sum(1 for word in context_words if word in full_text)
    
    # Must have a sport indicator AND context
    return (has_sport or has_position) and context_count >= 1

# Find genuine sports articles only
sports_articles = []

for article in all_articles:
    if is_genuine_sports_article(article):
        sports_articles.append({
            'title': article.get('title'),
            'description': article.get('description'),
            'desc_length': len(article.get('description', '')),
            'article_id': article.get('article_id')
        })

# Sort by description length
sports_articles_sorted = sorted(sports_articles, key=lambda x: x['desc_length'], reverse=True)

# Get the genuine sports article with longest description
if sports_articles_sorted:
    longest_sports = sports_articles_sorted[0]
    result = {
        'title': longest_sports['title'],
        'description_length': longest_sports['desc_length']
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No sports articles found'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

longest_sports_article.title: Pretty Log4J
longest_sports_article.description: \\I've been a big fan of Log4J  for a while now but haven't migrated any code\over for one central reason.  The following line of code:\\    final static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "some.name" );\\... is amazingly ugly and difficult to work with.\\Most people use Log4J with a logger based on the classname:\\So we would probably see:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( "org.apache.commons.feedparser.locate.FeedLocator" );\\Which is amazingly verbose.  A lot of developers shorten this to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.getLogger( FeedLocator.class );\\But this still leaves us with cut and paste errors.\\What if we could just reduce it to:\\    static Logger logger = Logger.g ...\\

longest_sports_article.title: Why Windows isn't Unix
longest_sports_article.description: \\"I first heard about this from one of the developers of the hit game SimCity, who\told me that there was a critical bug in his application: it used memory right\after freeing it, a major no-no that happened to work OK on DOS but would not\work under Windows where memory that is freed is likely to be snatched up by\another running application right away. The testers on the Windows team were\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. They reported this to the Windows\developers, who disassembled SimCity, stepped through it in a debugger, found\the bug, and added special code that checked if SimCity was running, and if it\did ...\\

matching_articles.title: Wash. State Team's Private Rocket Explodes (AP)
matching_articles.description: AP - A team taking a low-budget stab at the  #36;10 million Ansari X Prize for private manned spaceflight suffered a setback Sunday, when their rocket malfunctioned and exploded after shooting less than 1,000 feet in the air.

matching_articles.title: Canadian Team Joins Rocket Launch Contest (AP)
matching_articles.description: AP - The  #36;10 million competition to send a private manned rocket into space started looking more like a race Thursday, when a Canadian team announced plans to launch its rocket three days after an American group intends to begin qualifying for the Ansari X prize.

matching_articles.title: News: NASA Extends TRMM Operations Through 2004 Hurricane Season
matching_articles.description: NASA will extend operation of the Tropical Rainfall\Measuring Mission (TRMM) through the end of 2004, in light of a\recent request from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric\Administration (NOAA).

matching_articles.title: DVD player profits down to \$1
matching_articles.description: Want to get into the market for DVD players? Intense competition and standardization mean that you might make more as a mime.

matching_articles.title: International Group Teams Against Spam
matching_articles.description: Task force plans antispam campaign, from education to cross-border legal efforts.

matching_articles.title: Guitar Player Honors GarageBand
matching_articles.description: Guitar Player magazine announced during the summer NAMM show in Nashville that it has awarded GarageBand the magazine #146;s reader #146;s choice for Best Software of 2004. Jul 29

matching_articles.title: USC Begins Season Where It Ended, at No. 1 (AP)
matching_articles.description: AP - Southern California will begin defense of its first national title in 31 years as the No. 1 team in the nation. The Trojans earned the top spot in the preseason Associated Press poll released Saturday by receiving 48 of 65 first-place votes and 1,603 points.

matching_articles.title: Tiger Runs Out of Steam After Storming Start
matching_articles.description: KOHLER, Wisconsin (Reuters) - Tiger Woods failed to make  the most of a red-hot start in the U.S. PGA Championship third  round on Saturday, having to settle for a three-under-par 69.

matching_articles.title: AL Wrap: Olerud Cheers Yankees by Sinking Ex-Team
matching_articles.description: NEW YORK (Reuters) - John Olerud sunk his former team by  recording a two-run single in the eighth inning to drive in the  go-ahead runs which earned the New York Yankees a 6-4 win over  the host Seattle Mariners in the American League Saturday.

matching_articles.title: Phelps On Relay Team
matching_articles.description: Michael Phelps is named to the 4x100-meter freestyle relay team that will compete in Sunday's final, keeping alive his quest for a possible eight Olympic gold medals.

matching_articles.title: New Computer Games Teach Nutrition to Needy
matching_articles.description: In an effort to educate the nation's neediest children on nutrition, a new project uses the familiar medium of video games to broadcast its message. The Fantastic Food Challenge, a package of four computer games, is designed to teach people who get nutrition aid such as federal food stamps how to make better use of their food.

matching_articles.title: U.S. Softball Team Puts Australia in Its Place
matching_articles.description: ATHENS (Reuters) - The United States kept itself firmly on  course for a third straight Olympic softball gold medal when it  thrashed Australia 10-0 Sunday.

matching_articles.title: US NBA players become the Nightmare Team after epic loss (AFP)
matching_articles.description: AFP - Call them the "Nightmare Team".

matching_articles.title: U.S. Basketball Team Loses to Puerto Rico
matching_articles.description: ATHENS (Reuters) - The United States lost their first  basketball match at the Olympics since 1988 on Sunday when  Puerto Rico gave them a shock 92-73 trouncing.

top_5_longest.title: Why Windows isn't Unix
top_5_longest.description: \\"I first heard about this from one of the developers of the hit game SimCity, who\told me that there was a critical bug in his application: it used memory right\after freeing it, a major no-no that happened to work OK on DOS but would not\work under Windows where memory that is freed is likely to be snatched up by\another running application right away. The testers on the Windows team were\going through various popular applications, testing them to make sure they\worked OK, but SimCity kept crashing. They reported this to the Windows\developers, who disassembled SimCity, stepped through it in a debugger, found\the bug, and added special code that checked if SimCity was running, and if it\did ...\\

top_5_longest.title: I Confess.  I'm a Software Pirate.
top_5_longest.description: \\I'm guilty.  I'm a Software Pirate!  Not just one or two copies of DOS but\probably hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of software.\\Growing up my parents didn't have much money.  Certainly not hundred of\thousands of dollars for me to blow on software.  I was curious and had a\passion for computers that I couldn't control.  I simply wanted to learn and\couldn't afford to pay for software.\\Luckily I did this when I was a kid so hopefully I won't be prosecuted.  I also\believe that everything I did was ethical.  I didn't take any money out of the\hands of the software industry and I've already contributed WAY more to the\industry than a few hundred thousand in software sales.\\This is th ...\\

top_5_longest.title: What would Baby Jesus Think?
top_5_longest.description: \\"On Tuesday, Cheney, serving in his role as president of the Senate, appeared in\the chamber for a photo session. A chance meeting with Sen. Patrick J. Leahy\(Vt.), the ranking Democrat on the Judiciary Committee, became an argument about\Cheney's ties to Halliburton Co., an international energy services corporation,\and President Bush's judicial nominees. The exchange ended when Cheney offered\some crass advice.\\'Fuck yourself,' said the man who is a heartbeat from the presidency."\\Wonder what the moral majority has to say about this?\\It isn't profanity!  Its a freedom praise!\\

longest_sports_article.title: China Begins Countdown for Next Manned Space Flight
longest_sports_article.description: By ELAINE KURTENBACH    SHANGHAI, China (AP) -- Chinese astronauts are in the final stages of preparing for a manned space mission that will orbit the globe 14 times before returning to Earth, a state-run newspaper reported Thursday.    The launch, expected sometime this month, will initially send a manned craft, the Shenzhou 5, into an oval orbit that at its closest will be 125 miles from Earth, the Liberation Daily reported, citing "relevant channels."    After circling the earth several times, the ship will enter an orbit at about 220 miles from earth, the report said...
